title: Update calls in e2e to address 1101
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Update a couple of copy.Image() calls that were changed in #1097 that somehow didn't get flagged in its CI testing. 

Addresses #1101 and hopefully gets the CI tests running again.

comments.body:
@nalind @rhatdan 
About half the Travis runs failed with:
```
n -sf ../../opencontainers/runc/runc
Error: source files are not formatted according to recommendations. Run "gofmt -s -w" on:
./imagebuildah/build.go
make: *** [validate] Error 1
```
I'd not included that file with this PR.  I did the gofmt -s -w as noted, no changes to the file, added, commited, rebased, repushed.  Still no file changes showing for that one here.  I'm not positive the tests will pass again, I'm thinking imagebuildah/build.go might be scrozzled.  Thoughts on what to do?

:pushpin: Commit 1c4fb9e has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 1c4fb9e38cb79a2142c5f1694ad3c6b68ded7c5f -->
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.

Different versions of the toolchain can make changes to the default output of `go fmt` (both [1.10](https://golang.org/doc/go1.10#gofmt) and [1.11](https://golang.org/doc/go1.11#gofmt) have), so tests that expect our sources to match what it would produce should probably only be run for a single version of Go.  It's probably just luck that we didn't have logic that was affected by this earlier.
